item.title: Essendon announces selection panel to pick next AFL coach
item.description: Essendon has opted to go in-house with the panel that will decide whether James Hird returns as its AFL senior coach.

item.title: New residential proposal at Hobart's regatta grounds not supported by stadium developer
item.description: A proposal to build an underground car park, apartments and retail spaces neighbouring the Macquarie Point stadium site "does not appear to align with the precinct plan", stadium developer says.

item.title: Voice head calls for more funding while demonstrating ongoing success
item.description: South Australia's First Nations Voice to Parliament reforms are being revealed.

item.title: Former Coffs Harbour strata manager Jessica Marrie Carah charged with fraud
item.description: Jessica Marrie Carah is alleged to have transferred more than $2 million out of strata trust accounts.

item.title: Echuca Moama Taxi Group fined over deaths of two passengers in wheelchairs
item.description: A Victorian taxi company has been fined over the separate 2021 deaths of two passengers who used wheelchairs.

item.title: Youth offender program provider Namu Collective referred to authorities over alleged misappropriation of public funds
item.description: Namu Collective was awarded a contract last year for the government's "staying on track" program to stop youth offenders from falling back into the cycle of crime.

item.title: Broncos coach rejects claim season is done despite seventh straight loss
item.description: Queensland halfback Sam Walker is put on report for a shoulder charge in the Roosters' 24-18 win over Brisbane, potentially ruling him out of State of Origin III.

item.title: Judge finds mental health issues spurred grandmother's roadside stabbing attack
item.description: A 60-year-old woman who stabbed her grandson and daughter-in-law has been sentenced to jail. Her defence team had argued she was suffering sudden and extreme anger partially induced by weight-loss medication.

item.title: Canberra wildlife sanctuary builds quarantine pens amid fears H5 bird flu could 'decimate' quoll population
item.description: A population of endangered eastern quolls is being protected by a sanctuary in Canberra, but the threat of bird flu could mean that protection does not go far enough.

item.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics
item.description: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Former Hobart Hurricanes cricketer changes plea to guilty over underage sexual abuse charges
item.description: Aaron William Summers was playing for the Hobart Hurricanes in January 2018 when the underage sexual abuse occurred, a Tasmanian court has heard.

item.title: Star Hawk hurt as Giants count the injury cost of stinging loss
item.description: The Hawks hold on at the MCG to beat GWS and lock up third spot for the moment, in a game marred by a number of big injuries.

item.title: Detectives probe man's death in police presence and woman's death in crash near Meekatharra
item.description: Detectives are probing the deaths of a woman in a crash and a man who was found dead in a hotel a day later.

item.title: Ukraine launches heavy drone attack on Russia, annexed Crimea declares 'emergency'
item.description: Authorities in Russian-annexed Crimea have declared an "emergency situation".

item.title: Venue merch cuts are still putting artists at a loss, what needs to change?
item.description: Three years after a defining flashpoint in the dispute over merchandise cuts, venues and artists are still at an impasse.

item.title: Search for love takes singles off the apps and into the bush for old-school romance
item.description: Forget swiping right, singles are being urged to head to the bush in their search for real-life romance.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Bluey's friends, a glam rock classic and where to look for rainbows
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Vial of unapproved peptide retatrutide had double the strength indicated on label
item.description: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

item.title: Government to shake up to home loan subsidy scheme for Defence force members and veterans
item.description: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

item.title: The Interview: John Williamson on what it means to be True Blue in 2026
item.description: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

item.title: How on earth has the global economy survived this ongoing war?
item.description: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

item.title: Water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre 'best' locals have seen
item.description: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

item.title: The deadly H5 bird flu is here. These numbers show the devastation overseas
item.description: The deadly H5 bird flu has finally hit Australia. Here's how other nations have coped with the virus.

item.title: Can David Farley deliver on One Nation's promises after Farrer residents voted for change?
item.description: Farrer residents decisively voted to change their once-safe seat. They are now putting Pauline Hanson and One Nation to the test.

item.title: Family urges Voluntary Assisted Dying law reform to give dementia patients right to die
item.description: Families and dementia patients say they should be able to die on their own terms, so why isn't dementia treated like other incurable diseases? What would it take to change Voluntary Assisted Dying laws?

item.title: Beer follows wine industry's lead with terroir-driven ales
item.description: A boutique South Australian malt house is producing flavour-focused malt from barley grown in different regions.

item.title: H5N1 bird flu a deadly threat to native duck species already menaced from all angles
item.description: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

item.title: Hikers lost in Kosciuszko National Park rescued by drones powered by AI
item.description: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

item.title: Why a remote north Qld boat ramp became the centre of an international drug seizure investigation
item.description: Inside the journey of how the drugs from Australia's biggest seizure reached our shores.

item.title: Study results prove First Nations midwifery program leads to healthier babies
item.description: First Nations babies born through a midwifery program offered at three Melbourne hospitals are more likely to be full term and a healthy weight, a study has found.

item.title: Olive growers excited as consumer demand rises, industry looks to expand
item.description: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.

item.title: Queensland conservationists brace for potential bird flu battle
item.description: Home to the highest number of bird species in Australia, the sunshine state could have the most to lose from a bird flu outbreak.

item.title: Experts warn the polyphagous shot-hole borer could wreak havoc on Brisbane's parks if it reaches the east coast
item.description: A sesame seed-sized pest has destroyed 4,800 trees in Perth. Experts warn Brisbane's hot climate and dense tree population could be the perfect breeding ground for the pest.

item.title: Australian Maya Joint drawn to play Serena Williams in first round of Wimbledon, Alex de Minaur gets Roman Andres Burruchag
item.description: Australia's Maya Joint will take centre stage at Wimbledon after she was drawn to face Serena Williams in the opening round.

item.title: NSW budget another 'missed opportunity' for First Nations communities
item.description: There have been three state budgets since the failed Voice referendum in October 2023, and this year marked the fourth for the Minns Labor government.

item.title: Art 'like medicine' for accountant turned painter after brain injury
item.description: After undergoing brain surgery, Cheryl Blacklock can no longer process numbers quickly and has instead found a new life through art, painting faces that appear as visions in her mind.

item.title: Dung beetle exchange between South Australia and Tasmania ensures manure is managed all year round
item.description: While some dung beetles rest, others get to work, and farmers are making trades.

item.title: Ireland stuns India with historic 34-run T20 international win in Dublin
item.description: Ireland records its first ever international victory over India with a 34-run T20 win in Dublin.

item.title: NT Labor MLA Manuel Brown’s driving infringements further fuel party turmoil
item.description: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

item.title: John didn't realise he had pancreatic cancer until he taught a workshop on the disease. This is his message
item.description: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

item.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is the wonderkid dubbed God's gift to Indian cricket
item.description: On tour with India and on the brink of his international debut, the pressure is being heaped on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

item.title: Double brick dominates Perth homes. Experts say it could be slowing down construction
item.description: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

item.title: World Cup: Ousmane Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens
item.description: Ousmane Dembélé showed patience and a command of time and space, to help propel France to the top of its group, and remain on course for World Cup glory.

item.title: Portrait exhibition showcases stories of southern WA's Menang elders
item.description: A photography exhibition in Albany shares the stories of Menang elders, who often have been overlooked in history.

item.title: How boxer Hussein Dehaini became the latest casualty of Melbourne's underworld conflict
item.description: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

item.title: Polygamous sect leader Samuel Bateman convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway
item.description: A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year prison sentence in the US has been convicted of child abuse after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona.

item.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake - ABC News
item.description: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

item.title: Witness captures aircraft fragments and building damage in Beijing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sacred Ashura tradition a show of 'resistance' in Beirut amidst Israel-Hezbollah conflict
item.description: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

item.title: Attempted copper theft from Energex leaves 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power
item.description: Energex says it appears someone broke into a substation's cable pit and attempted to cut through an 11,000-volt cable, leaving about 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power overnight.

item.title: Butters coy about Power future after starring in upset Showdown win
item.description: Following a masterful display, Zak Butters is awarded the Showdown Medal in what may be his last match for the Power against the Crows.

item.title: Interactive exhibition encourages patrons to colour in art themselves
item.description: An art studio in Ballarat is hosting an interactive exhibition around a colouring book and visitors can contribute to the art.

item.title: Rich and famous families teach their kids Mandarin as China's influence grows
item.description: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

item.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching, grooming child under 16
item.description: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

item.title: Pete Buttigieg victim of false anonymous report alleging he was a 'danger' to his children
item.description: High profile Democrat Pete Buttigieg has been the victim of a false anonymous report, alleging he was a 'danger' to his four-year-old twins.

item.title: Panthers coach gives 13-word press conference after consecutive losses
item.description: Penrith Panthers coach Ivan Cleary has said just 13 words in his post-match press conference after the NRL leaders fell to consecutive defeats for the first time this season.

item.title: Australian Simon Carman arrested after body of teenage girl found in suitcase in Thailand
item.description: A 45-year-old Australian man is in custody as investigations continue into the discovery of the body of a teenage girl found stuffed inside a suitcase.

item.title: Crime scene declared after man shot by Queensland police north of Beaudesert
item.description: A man has been shot by police south of Brisbane after he allegedly confronted officers while carrying a rifle.

item.title: CCTV video shows an Australian man with a Thai girl who has died.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian tourist arrested over alleged murder of Thai girl found inside suitcase
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Citizen scientists say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice
item.description: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

item.title: Australian man arrested in Thailand after teenage girl found in suitcase
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Daughter of Torrington stabbing victims calls for better mobile coverage
item.description: The daughter of an elderly couple attacked in an alleged home invasion in northern NSW wants to see mobile coverage improved across regional areas.

item.title: Humpback whale entangled off NSW coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Satellite tracker monitors humpback whale entangled in rope and buoys off Sydney
item.description: An entangled humpback whale near Sydney is one of eight currently caught in marine debris off the New South Wales coast.

item.title: Vixens reach third straight Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Reigning champions the Melbourne Vixens will get a chance to defend their 2025 Super Netball title after beating the West Coast Fever 62-51in the preliminary final.

item.title: Guy Montgomery is back with your weekly spelling bee fix
item.description: Feel free to exhale that breath you've been holding in — it's Sunday and that means it's time to get spelling again.

item.title: Best books out in June, including Whistler by Ann Patchett and Land by Maggie O'Farrell
item.description: June's best books include an Irish saga by Maggie O'Farrell, a gritty memoir by Ralph Jackman and a comic Italian romp by Andrew Sean Greer.

item.title: A brain disease is striking down Aussie Rules players. At 23, Nick is the youngest
item.description: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie Rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's 'weird personality' might score him a Logie
item.description: Karl Stefanovic is no longer part of the Nine family but his audience may not be ready to abandon their prodigal son.

item.title: The truth about narcissists — and why most people get it wrong
item.description: As "narcissist" becomes the catch-all insult of the social media age, here is the crucial difference between everyday selfishness and a serious personality disorder.

item.title: Keith Urban on Tamworth, yacht rock and being an Aussie on the world stage
item.description: Keith Urban explains why he made a yacht rock album and how his Brisbane cover band days shaped Flow State.

item.title: Hanson's monoculture comments are from her three-step playbook and the Liberals played right into it
item.description: We've come a long way since it was Pauline Hanson who didn't know the meaning of big words.

item.title: How the Singha beer family is being torn apart by assault allegations
item.description: The billionaire family behind the Singha beer company is being torn apart by explosive allegations of assault and a century-old law that protects parents from neglectful children.

item.title: Electricity price spikes and batteries drain as wind drought tests green flag-bearer
item.description: South Australia is at the bleeding edge of the energy transition but record low winds have pushed its grid to the brink of its limits.

item.title: Lindy Chamberlain murder trial artefacts and memorabilia paint picture of frenzy around 1982 case
item.description: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

item.title: Central Queensland family's 'heartbreaking' fight against motor neurone disease
item.description: A Queensland family shares its journey with motor neurone disease for the first time and gives advice on how to cope with a diagnosis.

item.title: Naracoorte 'thriving' after town welcomes economic and social contribution of migrants
item.description: The township of Naracoorte, nestled in South Australia's rural heartland, has put out the welcome mat for migrants, who have helped enrich its social fabric and boost the economy.

item.title: The fastest human-powered Everest ascent from sea level
item.description: Avalanches, altitude sickness, orcs and Justin Bieber — a Brisbane mountaineer is believed to have set a new world record on Mount Everest for charity, despite every obstacle thrown his way.

item.title: Parents of children with amputations turn to online communities for accessible clothing
item.description: Braxton's mum says there needs to be more clothing options available for children with amputations, after struggling to find some for her son whose leg became amputated in the womb.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she has growing support in Canberra. But does it pass the pub test?
item.description: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

item.title: FIFA World Cup history beckons for Socceroos against 'fearless' Egypt in round of 32
item.description: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.

item.title: Young people from Australian and American cities find a home in outback Queensland
item.description: After escaping the big smoke, these five young people have swapped the metro rat race for a slower life in the outback – and say they wouldn’t change a thing.

item.title: Successful cat trapping effort leads to spike in Tasmanian devil population
item.description: A successful feral cat trapping effort in northern Tasmania leads to a spike in the devil population.

item.title: Chris Perry pushing for golf to feature in 2032 Paralympic Games
item.description: A Sunshine Coast man who was told he would never walk again after a bicycle accident now wants golf to be included in the 2032 Paralympic Games, crediting the sport with his recovery.

item.title: Serena Williams's return to Wimbledon sparks fan frenzy, praise from Novak Djokovic
item.description: The 23-time grand slam champion is mobbed by fans and praised by players as she returns to the All England Club ahead of her comeback match.

item.title: Key remote bird observatory shut for months before Australia's first bird flu detection
item.description: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

item.title: Communities turn to barter-style crop swaps to combat cost-of-living crisis
item.description: Bartering is one of the oldest trade systems in the world, and as the cost of living soars, there is growing interest in trading homegrown and homemade goods.

item.title: NSW shark-spotting drone program expanded after Coogee Beach shark attack
item.description: Shark-spotting drones will provide year-round coverage at about 70 beaches along the NSW coast as part of an expanded surveillance program announced after a Sydney mother was attacked by a suspected white shark.

item.title: Curtin University program helps connect young autistic people to cyber security industry
item.description: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

item.title: Wallabies and All Blacks to play Bledisloe Cup Tests on Anzac Day at Lang Park
item.description: For the first time in 123 years, the Wallabies and All Blacks will play a Bledisloe Cup Test on Anzac Day, with Lang Park hosting in 2027, 2029 and 2031.

item.title: Long-awaited Perth-to-Bunbury train service reopens
item.description: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

item.title: England through to knock out rounds, but flatter to deceive as pre-World Cup plans start to falter
item.description: England came into the World Cup with a side it thought was capable of beating the best teams in the world. If it can't beat the little guys, it may never get a chance to prove its theory right.

item.title: Beyond the Shed training program prepares WA shearers for their future and a stronger local workforce
item.description: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.

item.title: Thai teenager's family grieves as Australian charged with murder says death was 'out of my control'
item.description: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

item.title: Love story: Holding onto the glimmers of my dad, Blues legend Steve Mortimer
item.description: Erin Mortimer's dad, Bulldogs and Blues legend Steve Mortimer, was diagnosed with dementia at just 60 after years of concussions during his playing career. Erin is holding on to the special memories, and the glimmers of him that remain.

item.title: Bali's beauty sinks beneath an 'Island of Trash' as waste issues increase
item.description: Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers promises to 'fix' property tax oversight to protect widows and divorcees
item.description: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Salah's injury a Socceroos boost, Scotland's coach resigns and the tightest of offsides
item.description: The Socceroos' next World Cup opponents Egypt are sweating on the fitness of key striker Mohamed Salah, Scotland's coach Steve Clarke resigns after his team's exit and Davinson Sánchez was denied a late winner because of his toe. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Queensland police conduct counterterrorism training at shopping centres, stadiums
item.description: A scenario based on the deadly Bondi Junction shopping centre stabbings in 2024 has been used to test officers as part of specialist counterterrorism training.

item.title: New parents struggling with anxiety and depression urged to seek free counselling services
item.description: New parents often struggle in silence, but a growing number of perinatal clinics offering free mental health support are being rolled out across Australia.

item.title: Man charged after four-month-old baby's death in 2024
item.description: The 22-year-old Bundaberg man has been charged with domestic violence offences of murder and torture.

item.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
item.description: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

item.title: Blues ring in the changes as To'o dumped for decider
item.description: New South Wales coach Laurie Daley has made five changes to the Blues side for Origin III with Brian To'o missing out for the first time in over five years.

item.title: World Cup: Who plays who in round of 32? Who do the Socceroos face? What time in Australia?
item.description: The World Cup has reached the round of 32. Here is the full fixture list for the remainder of the tournament, including the kick-off times in Australia.

item.title: Face paint, chants and flags — the many ways football fans celebrate the World Cup
item.description: From donning face paint to viral chants, each fandom has its peculiarities.

item.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

item.title: The surprising reasons some bosses oppose work from home
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sydney and Melbourne record worst property auction clearance results in years
item.description: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

item.title: Thousands gather for iconic Beer Can Regatta at Darwin's Mindil Beach
item.description: Competitors at the annual Beer Can Regatta race rafts engineered from drinking containers, with beer or soft-drink cans the popular choice this year.

item.title: Father of Thai girl found in suitcase shares grief
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: How the final qualification place was decided by 90 minutes of chaos between Algeria and Austria
item.description: Two stoppage-time goals, ghosts of a World Cup scandal from 42 years ago, and a desperately unlucky Iranian side are just some of the takeaways from Algeria and Austria's chaotic group stage finale.

item.title: WA parents say they 'deserve' answers after baby dies from 'unknown cause'
item.description: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

item.title: Australian run of Waitress closing early with Sydney shows cancelled
item.description: The Australian stage production of Waitress will no longer continue on its national tour, with planned shows in Sydney now cancelled.

item.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board
item.description: A small aircraft carrying skydivers has crashed in eastern France, killing all 11 people on board, local authorities say.

item.title: England captain Ben Stokes to retire from international cricket after third Test vs New Zealand
item.description: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

item.title: Canada 'heroes' score stunning injury time winner to down South Africa
item.description: World Cup co-host Canada beats South Africa to progress into the last 16 thanks to a last-gasp strike from Stephen Eustaquio in Los Angeles.

item.title: This week in history quiz June 29: Test your smarts on Eminem, Jane Austen and competitive chess
item.description: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

item.title: Do you qualify for three hours of free electricity? Here's how it works
item.description: Under the federal government's Solar Sharer scheme, some Australian households will be able to access three hours of free electricity a day. Here's how you can make the most of it.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch the week four films
item.description: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

item.title: Gulf states 'colder, harder and more transactional' under US-Iran deal
item.description: Before a flare-up in fighting over the weekend, the interim deal between the United States and Iran was giving the region something it badly needs, analysts say.

item.title: Trump allies in the South are wiping out Black votes
item.description: States in America's South are scrambling to draw up new electoral maps that would eliminate majority-Black electorates.

item.title: False accusations and mistrust divide Iranian diaspora from within
item.description: Iranian Australians are grappling with mistrust, harassment and isolation within a community deeply traumatised by an oppressive regime.

item.title: Iran and China are simultaneously driving a major energy transition
item.description: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

item.title: As Taylor struggles to handle Hanson, Hastie talks tough on One Nation
item.description: While Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie has been talking tough on One Nation.

item.title: Dozens of Australian Rules players diagnosed with CTE expose scale of AFL's brain injury crisis
item.description: Australian Rules footballers who never played in the game's premier league are being diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis striking all levels of the game.

item.title: ASIC says too many Australians' retirement savings being wiped out
item.description: The corporate watchdog is concerned that super trustees that manage Australians' retirement savings have a lack of safeguards in place.

item.title: Coal and gas giants spending tens of millions on programs in schools and children's sport
item.description: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

item.title: Lawyers band together as Tasmania's court backlog hits 'crisis point'
item.description: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

item.title: Real estate has been an easy target for criminals laundering money, until now
item.description: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

item.title: Calls for Health Department to review fatigue policy after Tasmanian doctor dies in car crash
item.description: Doctors are calling for Tasmania's Department of Health to examine its fatigue management policies after a doctor died on the state's roads. Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".

item.title: Vanuatu critical infrastructure to remain 'free from militarisation' under Nakamal pact
item.description: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

item.title: The national program teaching boys as well as girls about endometriosis, periods and pelvic pain
item.description: Educators behind a national program teaching high school students about periods and pelvic pain hope it will encourage empathy.

item.title: Man charged with DV murder after woman shot on the Gold Coast
item.description: Ethan Midgely, 23, faces one domestic violence murder charge after he allegedly shot and killed who police believe was his de facto partner.

item.title: Veterans to get equal health benefits but ageing National Servicemen want priority
item.description: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

item.title: Customers in remote Australia have safety concerns as Telstra phases out CAN Radio phone system
item.description: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquakes rescuers save infant and two boys alive from rubble
item.description: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

item.title: Tech revival sees ASX close 0.7pc higher, Neuren leaps on EU approval hopes — as it happened
item.description: The ASX shrugged off a sluggish Wall Street lead to close 0.7 per cent higher as Neuren Pharmaceuticals surged on a potential EU approval for one of its drugs.

item.title: Queensland set for brief warm-up before weekend cold snap returns
item.description: Queensland will see a brief midweek warm-up with temperatures rising into the mid-20s in the south before a short-lived cold snap returns over the weekend.

item.title: Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carry Australia to T20 World Cup win over India
item.description: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

item.title: NT Police says new public safety officers on the beat from today will receive the same firearms training as constables
item.description: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

item.title: 'It's time': Liberal frontbencher calls for party to rebrand — as it happened
item.description: Liberal frontbencher Melissa McIntosh says her party needs a rebrand after support for the Coalition hit a new low in the latest opinion polls.

item.title: Mt Rawdon pumped hydro project shelved by Qld government
item.description: A $6 billion dollar project to rehabilitate a gold mine and turn it into a pumped hydro power station has been dumped by the Queensland government.

item.title: Maroons recall veteran for Origin decider after Blues' bombshell
item.description: Billy Slater names Patrick Carrigan in his Queensland squad for State of Origin III despite the Broncos forward having not played since late May due to an ankle injury.

item.title: Farmer's push to return lost CSIRO archives reveals 200 years of merino history
item.description: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction, scenes at Algeria's home away from home
item.description: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here's the five quick hits from the World Cup.

item.title: Socceroos to play Brazil at Lang Park in first home game after World Cup
item.description: The first of the two friendlies will be at Brisbane's Lang Park on September 25, and the second at Townsville on September 29.

item.title: Designer featured on Gardening Australia stripped of NDIS support funding
item.description: A central Victorian garden designer who lives with Duchenne muscular dystrophy says sudden changes to his NDIS plan put his life at risk.

item.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as Europe's record heatwave sparks power outages and wildfires
item.description: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

item.title: Cameron Myers wins Diamond League 1,500m, Sarah Billings runs 800m national record
item.description: Cameron Myers wins the 1,500m final at the Paris Diamond League event, joining 800m star Sarah Billings in setting a national record at the meet.

item.title: Spectacular lightning display at the Eiffel Tower
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: Has the chaotic, but engaging group stage justified the expanded tournament?
item.description: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

item.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
item.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

item.title: Hae-ran Ryu wins Women's PGA Championship, Karis Davidson 12th at Hazeltine Golf Club
item.description: South Korea's Hae-ran Ryu completes her comeback to win the PGA Championship and collect the biggest pay cheque in women's golf history.

item.title: US and Iran to halt strikes as technical talks set to continue
item.description: The US and Iran will stand down on strikes and vessels will be allowed to move freely, as technical talks are slated to continue on all areas of the memorandum of understanding.

item.title: Artist's impression of NASA's Swift Boost Mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NASA Swift rescue mission spacecraft will bump telescope back into orbit
item.description: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

item.title: Bondi terror attack survivor tells antisemitism royal commission he received 'influx of hate'
item.description: Deepfake and altered images depicting a Bondi Beach terror attack survivor holding an Academy Award while covered in blood spread "like wildfire" online just hours after the shooting, a royal commission has heard.

item.title: Crows star Chelsea Randall says concussion left with her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW
item.description: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

item.title: Father dead, 12-year-old son hospitalised following single vehicle crash in Blackmans Bay
item.description: A man has died, and his son remains in hospital, following a single-vehicle crash in Blackmans Bay in Tasmania's south.

item.title: WA regional courts to reopen a month earlier than planned after security issues
item.description: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

item.title: Allan government under pressure amid latest Big Build corruption allegations
item.description: The Victorian premier has backed her government's response to corruption in the construction industry, despite criticism from across politics and integrity experts.

item.title: Canberra man found guilty of attempted murder after stabbing his wife in violent home attack
item.description: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

item.title: Serena Williams criticises tennis anti-doping protocol ahead of Wimbledon return
item.description: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

item.title: Liberal MP Moira Deeming breaks silence after assault allegations against Matthew Guy
item.description: State Liberal MP Moira Deeming says she misunderstood the meaning of the term "headlock", after alleging her party room colleague Matthew Guy assaulted her.

item.title: Winton's film festival kicks off as Red Dog director reveals new thriller
item.description: Red Dog director Kriv Stenders reveals plans for a new thriller film as the annual Vision Splendid Outback Film Festival kicks off in Winton.

item.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of international fitness brand, dies aged 91
item.description: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

item.title: Father charged with murder and torture of four-month-old baby to remain in custody
item.description: Police say the baby's condition was consistent with "a child-shaking injury".

item.title: Outback roads badly damaged by flooding could take year to fix
item.description: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

item.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA
item.description: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

item.title: Health minister declines to request coronial inquest into baby Willow Horne's death in Kalgoorlie
item.description: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

item.title: Superannuation switching trend sees billions flow out of retail funds, at a risk
item.description: There is now more than $1 trillion in self-managed superannuation funds, which means a big chunk of money isn't regulated by APRA, and it's the rate of the shift into SMSFs and less-regulated managed investment schemes that has regulators worried.

item.title: Stobie pole vandals put public at risk of electrocution in Adelaide's north
item.description: Members of the public were put at "severe risk" of electrocution after four Stobie poles were vandalised in Adelaide's north in the past month, causing some to fall and expose high voltage electrical lines.

item.title: Calls for SA drivers to slow down and pay attention amid spate of fatal crashes
item.description: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

item.title: Man found dead after fire engulfs bus on Tallygaroopna property
item.description: Police say emergency services arrived at the Tallygaroopna property to find the fire had engulfed a bus, which appears to have been used as a permanent home.

item.title: Missing Darkan woman found safe after five-day community-led search
item.description: The desperate search for a 46-year-old woman who disappeared in southern Western Australia last week ends with her being found safe and well.

item.title: Broncos winger Josiah Karapani stood down after alleged drink driving, traffic offences
item.description: In a statement, the club confirmed the 24-year-old had been issued with a notice to appear in court and would not be available for the next NRL game.

item.title: Four quick and easy dinners using inexpensive chickpeas
item.description: From comforting curry to hearty soup, these easy recipes will get chickpeas out of the pantry and onto the menu.

item.title: Teenagers charged after alleged crime spree in Melbourne's south
item.description: The pair are alleged to have robbed fellow teens in Frankston and Cranbourne while driving a stolen car before being arrested in Dandenong.

item.title: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics are opening in Canberra but there are already questions about long-term viability
item.description: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics are opening in Canberra, but questions remain about how they will be funded and staffed long-term.

item.title: Lauryn Hill honoured with Living Legend Icon Award at BET Awards
item.description: American musician and actress Lauryn Hill has been recognised with the inaugural Living Legend Icon Award at the BET Awards by the Black Entertainment Television network in the United States.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley to answer critics after State of Origin III against Maroons
item.description: Laurie Daley says he will address criticism about his performance as Blues coach once the State of Origin series is over.

item.title: The Brunette Races in pictures: Jillaroos and jackaroos flock to iconic NT event
item.description: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

item.title: Alleged Russian spies Kira and Igor Korolev charged with new espionage offence
item.description: Crown prosecutors presented a new bench charge for the couple of conspiracy to commit espionage.

item.title: Queensland state electorates of Stretton and Hill to be abolished after review
item.description: Significant changes were made to 50 of Queensland's 93 electorates by the independent Queensland Redistribution Commission (QRC).

item.title: Brother tells of family's 'unimaginable pain' after doctor's death in Tasmania
item.description: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

item.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage
item.description: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has been knocked back in a bid to gain access to raw documentary footage which featured Brittany Higgins.

item.title: Alleged victim of William 'Little Pebble' Kamm tells court of fear of escaping cult
item.description: An alleged victim of cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm has told a court she began experiencing extreme anxiety as she pondered an escape from a property on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Billy Slater reveals stance on Maroons coaching future ahead of State of Origin III
item.description: Billy Slater says he will not sign another contract to be Queensland State of Origin coach, but that does not mean he will not continue in the role.

item.title: Network 10 agrees to temporary deal with WIN Television in country SA and NSW
item.description: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

item.title: WA Police stumped by apparent heist of 8 tonnes of tugboat propellers
item.description: Thieves have allegedly made away from a WA mining town with 8 tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

item.title: Aidan Becker plaque unveiled to remember man who died at Mernda train station while trying to save teenager
item.description: The plaque remembers 22-year-old Aidan Becker, who was killed when trying to rescue a 14-year-old boy from an alleged machete attack at Mernda train station in March.

item.title: ICAC hears UOW executive was dismissed the day he rejected colleague's pay rise
item.description: The ICAC inquiry has heard a former University of Wollongong executive was dismissed on the day of a disputed salary increase.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Teen arrested twice in three days for alleged police pursuits in stolen cars
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Seven teenagers were arrested after an alleged police persuit involving two stolen cars
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police union calls for tougher bail laws for repeat offenders after string of stolen car pursuits
item.description: A 14-year-old boy charged twice in three days after allegedly leading police on stolen car pursuits is among several incidents prompting the police union to call for tougher bail laws in the ACT.

item.title: Anthony Albanese defends both multiculturalism and budget changes to capital gains tax
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese issues a defence of CGT changes from the budget and Australia's multicultural society.

item.title: Vladimir Putin says Russia will press on with front-line campaign despite fuel shortages
item.description: President Vladimir Putin has said Russia will press ahead with its battlefield aim of fully capturing four Ukrainian regions, rejecting diplomatic overtures from Ukraine.
